articles.title: Investors still “largely downbeat” about renewables, as policy and fossil risks overshadow rewards
articles.summary:
Headline policy reform has not translated into improved investment conditions for renewables in Australia, a new survey has found, with 20% saying things have got worse.
The post Investors still “largely downbeat” about renewables, as policy and fossil risks overshadow rewards appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: “Pouring oil on climate fire:” Global fossil fuel use must halve by 2035 to avoid catastrophic climate damage
articles.summary:
Global fossil fuel use must halve by 2035 and be phased out entirely by 2070 at the latest if the world is to keep global warming below 1.5°C.
The post “Pouring oil on climate fire:” Global fossil fuel use must halve by 2035 to avoid catastrophic climate damage appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: Must do better: Bowen seeks rule change to force energy retailers to do right thing by electricity customers
articles.summary:
Federal energy minister seeks principles-based rule change to ensure retailers are doing more than just the bare minimum to engage with electricity customers.
The post Must do better: Bowen seeks rule change to force energy retailers to do right thing by electricity customers appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: Contested wind project pivots turbines and cuts footprint after discovering more endangered cycads
articles.summary:
Wind farm developer has shaved 110 hectares off its footprint after working with EPBC planners to improve the environmental credentials of the contested project.
The post Contested wind project pivots turbines and cuts footprint after discovering more endangered cycads appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: SwitchedOn podcast: The hidden energy guzzler in Australian backyards – and how it could help the grid
articles.summary:
Australia’s backyard pools could help support a cleaner and more flexible electricity grid and save households hundreds of dollars a year.
The post SwitchedOn podcast: The hidden energy guzzler in Australian backyards – and how it could help the grid appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: First of a kind “carbon refinery” to embed emissions in concrete and other building materials
articles.summary:
An Australian facility will seek to prove carbon can be embedded into useful products, such as concrete, paint and plasterboard, and resold for a profit.
The post First of a kind “carbon refinery” to embed emissions in concrete and other building materials appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: Naturgy boosts Australian solar portfolio with start-up of 360 MW of new capacity
articles.summary: Spanish energy giant Naturgy has increased its operational renewables portfolio in Australia to 1.3 GW with the commissioning of two large-scale solar farms with a combined capacity of 360 MW.

articles.title: Batteries and microgrids offer possible energy solution for edge-of-grid applications
articles.summary: Australia's big clean energy projects get a lot of attention, from giant solar farms to the Snowy 2.0 pumped hydro scheme. So too does the push for rooftop solar and home batteries.

articles.title: Strong support for government-backed renewable energy future
articles.summary: New polling shows 75% of Australians believe government should play a greater role in building and operating renewable energy infrastructure, with only a quarter of those polled believing privatisation of energy assets has been good for the country.

articles.title: NSW launches $15,000 zero-interest loans for rooftop solar, home batteries
articles.summary: The New South Wales government is now offering interest-free loans of up to $15,000 for eligible households to install energy-saving upgrades such as rooftop solar and battery energy storage systems.

articles.title: Southeast Asia’s BESS developers must ‘engage early with regulators, utilities and communities’
articles.summary: Developers of battery storage projects across Southeast Asia must heed lessons learned elsewhere and engage with key stakeholders as early as possible, Energy-Storage.news has heard.

articles.title: Zero interest loans launched to help lower income homes get off gas with solar, batteries, insulation
articles.summary:
State government launches Home Energy Saver program offering zero-interest loans of up to $15,000 to help pay for upgrades including rooftop solar, batteries, electric appliances and insulation.
The post Zero interest loans launched to help lower income homes get off gas with solar, batteries, insulation appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: New Zealand dairy giant signs two major solar deals as it works to wean itself off coal boilers
articles.summary:
World's biggest dairy exporter signs PPAs with two new solar projects as part of its efforts to wean itself off the use of coal power for boilers.
The post New Zealand dairy giant signs two major solar deals as it works to wean itself off coal boilers appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: Unique off-grid trial proves technical case for renewable hydrogen power, despite repeated fuel cell failures
articles.summary:
A unique trial of renewable hydrogen power in an off-grid coastal community has struggled because of repeated failures from the hydrogen fuel cell.
The post Unique off-grid trial proves technical case for renewable hydrogen power, despite repeated fuel cell failures appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: Chart of the Day: Farewell King Coal, long live King Solar (and wind and batteries)
articles.summary:
In capacity-addition terms, fossil fuels are now just a thin orange strip at the bottom of a very tall green wall.
The post Chart of the Day: Farewell King Coal, long live King Solar (and wind and batteries) appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: Hybrid solar-hydrogen project planned at Antarctic base
articles.summary: A hybrid power pilot project combining solar, batteries and hydrogen fuel cells is under development at a scientific base on King George Island to explore reducing diesel consumption in isolated research operations.

articles.title: Longi unveils 27.27%-efficient HJT back-contact solar cell built with improved laser processing
articles.summary: Researchers from Longi and Yangzhou University developed a laser-based manufacturing strategy to mitigate shock-wave-induced damage in HJT back-contact solar cells, improving passivation stability and device performance. The approach yields a certified 27.27% efficient solar cell and provides an industrially viable pathway toward ultra-high-efficiency photovoltaic fabrication.

articles.title: Sunergise flicks switch on NZ’s largest rooftop solar installation
articles.summary: New Zealand commercial solar company Sunergise has switched on the country’s largest-yet rooftop solar system, commissioning a 5.3 MWp array installed at an industrial site in Auckland.

articles.title: Spill baby spill: how to read the opportunity trapped in curtailment
articles.summary: Curtailment is information for capital. Especially if you understand the intersection between where curtailed energy exists, where flexibility is most valuable, network constraints and portfolio exposure.

articles.title: NSW’s $1 billion green bank backs big batteries with first investment
articles.summary: The New South Wales government’s new green bank has unveiled its first project, investing $100 million to support construction of a battery network expected to reach up to 1 GW of capacity by 2031.

articles.title: Silver drives PV recycling economics as module waste wave approaches
articles.summary: With Australia and Europe facing a significant wave of end-of-life solar panels, researchers point to silver recovery as the most important lever for improving unit economics, but technical barriers and the absence of industry-wide purity standards are slowing progress.

articles.title: Energy Dome, Invinity Energy Systems advance non-lithium LDES projects in the US
articles.summary: Energy Dome is advancing a 10-hour CO2 Battery project in Arizona, with SRP and Google, while Invinity announces the sale of North America’s largest VRFB, in California.

articles.title: AESC signs 10GWh US domestic content BESS cell and module deal with system integrator Prevalon
articles.summary: AESC has announced a strategic supply partnership with BESS system integrator Prevalon Energy.

articles.title: Ontario IESO procures 640MW energy storage in LT2 Request for Proposals
articles.summary: Ontario, Canada’s Independent Electricity System Operator (IESO) has secured 640MW of new capacity through three projects selected under the capacity stream of the Second Long-Term Request for Proposals (LT2).

articles.title: US Roundup: Virginia’s biggest standalone BESS, Cypress Creek raises US$3.5 billion and Kore sells mobile battery business
articles.summary: Virginia’s biggest standalone BESS comes online, Cypress Creek raises funding for a gigawatt-scale hybrid resource, and Kore Power’s mobile solutions subsidiary is sold, in this edition of news in brief.

articles.title: Western Power begins work on 18 new community batteries in Western Australia
articles.summary: Western Power has started construction on 18 community battery energy storage systems across Perth and Bunbury in Western Australia.

articles.title: Australia’s Energy Security Corporation’s debut investment backs 650MW Ausgrid battery storage platform
articles.summary: The NSW Energy Security Corporation has deployed its first investment, committing AU$100 million to a large-scale battery storage platform.

articles.title: Wärtsilä wraps energy storage business into joint venture with solar manufacturer RCT Solutions
articles.summary: There has been another twist in the long-running saga of Wärtsilä’s energy storage business and how the company aims to run it going forward.

articles.title: VIDEO: Germany’s ramp constraints, grid limitations and their impact on the energy storage business case
articles.summary: Energy-Storage.news presents this sponsored webinar with Clean Horizon, on how Germany’s grid connection agreements can affect the business case for battery storage projects.

articles.title: Inside South Australia’s FERM tender: Why a technology-neutral process produced an all-battery outcome
articles.summary: ESN Premium speaks with ASL on the latest FERM tender results in South Australia and why batteries excelled.

articles.title: APAC data centres risk a fossil fuel dependency long-duration energy storage can help end
articles.summary: APAC’s AI data centres look set to lock in a decade of coal and gas dependency, but long-duration energy storage can break that cycle, says Pavina Adunratanasee of ArkTerra Partners.

articles.title: Panasonic to convert Kansas EV battery factory for data centre applications
articles.summary: Japanese consumer electronics giant Panasonic intends to convert its electric vehicle (EV) battery cell manufacturing facility in Kansas, US, to produce batteries for data centre applications, beginning Q3 of 2029.

articles.summary: Legislation to create an incentive programme for behind-the-meter (BTM) energy storage in New Jersey has been advanced by the US state’s Senate Environment and Energy Committee.

articles.summary: Energy-Storage.news Premium speaks with Scott Blalock, general manager, integrated applications engineering, at BESS integrator Wärtsilä Energy Storage.

articles.title: Portion of Czech hydropower station to be transformed into 154MW/750MWh pumped storage plant
articles.summary: The Czech Republic's fourth pumped hydro energy storage plant is to be built within an existing hydropower complex, converting convention run-of-river into reversible units, creating 750MWh of energy storage capacity.

articles.title: RWE officially opens Australia’s first 8-hour battery storage system
articles.summary: RWE Renewables Australia has officially opened the 50MW/400MWh Limondale battery energy storage system (BESS) near Balranald in south-west New South Wales.

articles.title: Sembcorp completes US$4.32 billion acquisition of Alinta Energy, adds 10.4GW Australian pipeline
articles.summary: Sembcorp Industries has completed its acquisition of Alinta Energy at a value of AU$6.5 billion (US$4.32 billion).

articles.title: VDA calls for ‘staged approach’ to implement EU Battery Passport
articles.summary: A 'staged approach' to implementing the EU Battery Passport could be essential if Europe is to improve its battery supply chain.

articles.title: Greenvolt inaugurates Hungary’s largest BESS; pipelines progressed and financed elsewhere in EU
articles.summary: IPP Greenvolt has put a 99.8MW/288.6MWh BESS into commercial operation in Hungary, the largest in the country, while pipelines and projects have been progressed in Italy, France, Netherlands, Belgium and Spain.

articles.title: Europe is ‘walking into new dependencies’ in global battery supply chain
articles.summary: 'We are walking with open eyes into new dependencies,' said ReCharge's Ilka von Dalwigk at the Energy Storage Summit.

articles.summary: There is a big gap in the industry's understanding of the Spanish energy storage market, but that gap also creates opportunities for smart operators, writes Ignacio López Martín, chair of developer Capflex Energía.

articles.summary: Minimising counterparty risk is a key component of the German BESS sector, particularly amid ongoing political uncertainty.

articles.summary: The upcoming German capacity market must be designed in such a way that enables BESS to generate revenue, according to industry experts.

articles.summary: Developer EDF power solutions North America and developer-operator Abu Dhabi Future Energy Company – Masdar, have entered into 15-year power purchase agreements (PPAs) for a California, US solar-plus-storage project.

articles.summary: Virtual power plant (VPP) operator and distributed energy resource (DER) platform Voltus and tech giant Google have signed a three-year 100MW agreement for the PJM region in the US.

articles.title: Modelling approaches to detecting and preventing thermal runaway risk in Li-ion BESS
articles.summary: Liam Critchley examines the modelling, safety standards and analytics that aim to mitigate the risk of thermal runaway in lithium-ion battery energy storage systems (BESS).

articles.title: Electricity bills to fall even further than predicted in state as renewables share nears 50 pct
articles.summary:
State regulator sets default price of power even lower than projected in March, giving the renewables-forward state government a win with voters ahead of the November state election.

articles.title: Fortescue begins building 690 MW solar farm and 650 MWh battery system
articles.summary: One of Australia’s biggest miners has commenced construction of a 690 MW solar farm and 650 MWh battery energy storage system in Western Australia as part of plans to eliminate diesel and gas from its iron ore operations.

articles.title: Singapore team builds 19.7%-efficient ultrathin ‘biPoly’ TOPCon solar cell
articles.summary: The ultra-thin TOPCon solar cell uses dual-sided polysilicon passivated contacts formed by low-pressure chemical vapor deposition. The champion device, with a thickness of only 80 μm, achieved 19.7% efficiency with 719 mV open-circuit voltage and demonstrated strong passivation, optical performance, and mechanical flexibility.

articles.title: Record-dry April lifts irradiance across Eastern Australia
articles.summary: Solcast, a DNV company, reports that April 2026 was one of Australia’s driest Aprils in recent years, with widespread rainfall deficits driven by persistent high pressure and ENSO returning to neutral, leading to mostly clear skies and above-normal solar irradiance across much of the continent. New South Wales and Victoria saw the strongest sunshine surpluses, while remnants of ex-Tropical Cyclone Maila brought cloud and rain to the north, creating regional contrasts in rainfall and solar con...

articles.title: IRENA proposes global electrification target of 35% by 2035
articles.summary: New report from the International Renewable Energy Agency says renewables will be key to meeting rising global demand for electricity but with up to 2.5 TW of solar, storage and wind projects already in connection queues worldwide, increased investment in grid infrastructure will be pivotal.

articles.title: Data | Power: Navigating policy, regulation and networks
articles.summary: Australia is at a pivotal moment in its energy and digital transformation. The energy transition is driving electricity demand growth through electrification and unprecedented levels of investment in renewable generation.

articles.title: 22 solar projects share $1.9 million funding round for Solomon Islands communities
articles.summary: The Australian government has partnered with communities across the Solomon Islands to develop 22 solar powered streetlight and solar energy projects, through an inaugural program's $1.9 million funding round.

articles.title: Tasmania commits $1.8 billion for energy generation and asset upgrades
articles.summary: The 2026-27 Tasmania Budget and Forward Estimates has committed $1.8 billion for energy generation and network asset upgrades to future proof the state's electricity supply and Marinus Link connection.

articles.title: Spearmint Energy secures US$450 million for 600MWh Texas BESS project
articles.summary: Battery storage developer and operator Spearmint Energy has closed a US$450 million financing to support its 300MW/600MWh Red Egret battery energy storage system (BESS) project.

articles.title: LDES investor Frontier Power acquires 480MWh Texas BESS portfolio from Bimergen
articles.summary: Long-duration energy storage (LDES) development and investment company Frontier Power USA (FPUSA) has acquired a 480MWh battery energy storage system (BESS) portfolio from developer-operator Bimergen Energy.

articles.title: Germany market participants starting to price 2029-onwards projects despite uncertainty
articles.summary: The situation and uncertainty around grid connections and grid fees in Germany is evolving, possibly enabling market participants to look beyond the August 2029 grid fee exemption cut-off date - although uncertainty is still very high.

articles.title: EDF completes 120MWh Poland BESS; Eurus, NGEN and Aretis progress other CEE projects
articles.summary: EDF Power Solutions has commissioned a 50MW/120MWh BESS project in Poland, Eurus has done the same in Hungary, while NGEN and Aretis Group have financed and enlisted optimisers (respectively) for projects in Hungary, Romania, Poland, Slovenia and Latvia.

articles.title: Masdar picks Sungrow as supplier for world’s largest 24/7 renewable energy project in UAE
articles.summary: Masdar has selected Sungrow for the world’s first gigawatt-scale 24/7 dispatchable renewable energy project in the United Arab Emirates (UAE).

articles.title: UNSW researchers shed new light on UV-induced degradation in PERC, TOPCon solar cells
articles.summary: The scientists identified that UV exposure in PERC and TOPCon solar cells increases interface defects and makes them more recombination-active, driving ultraviolet-induced degradation. They found strong degradation on PERC front and rear surfaces and TOPCon front surfaces, while the TOPCon rear remains stable due to poly-Si UV absorption.

articles.title: 1KOMMA5° achieves 1 GW of shiftable load with its virtual power plant globally
articles.summary: 1KOMMA5° Australia is currently rolling out the Heartbeat AI technology while its European parent company has announced a 1 GW shiftable load milestone with its virtual power plant globally.

articles.title: Free electricity coming: are Aussies aware of Solar Sharer?
articles.summary: Australia is world-leading in solar adoption, with one in 3 homes equipped with a rooftop solar system. Ongoing volatility in fuel and energy prices is prompting Australian households to rethink how they power their homes and vehicles, with solar emerging as a way to buffer against rising costs.

articles.title: Microgrid developer granted $3 million to build clean energy ‘island’ blueprint
articles.summary: The first project funded under Stream A of ARENA's regional microgrid program has been awarded $3 million toward an overall $15 million cost to build a 7 MWp solar and 5 MW / 10 MWh battery storage system to island a New South Wales' community's energy security in the event of transmission failure.

articles.title: Grok Ventures backs 5 GWh thermal energy storage project in US
articles.summary: Grok Ventures has provided project-level financing as the sole external investor on a US-based 5 GWh thermal energy storage system providing 50 MW of round-the-clock energy through an offtake agreement with biofuels giant, POET.

articles.title: Developers must become ‘a bit of a firefighter, sound expert, and light expert’: Addressing BESS safety and community opposition
articles.summary: At a recent industry event hosted by market research firm Wood Mackenzie, energy storage experts discussed their approaches to public engagement, especially around addressing fire and other safety concerns.

articles.title: US ROUNDUP: BESS project advancements from OCI Energy, MN8 Energy, GridStor, Grenergy
articles.summary: In this US news roundup, OCI Energy, MN8 Energy, GridStor, and Grenergy advance battery energy storage system (BESS) projects in Texas, California, Colorado, and Georgia.
